ddls:
CREATE TABLE "signals" (
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each signal */,
  "signal_name" TEXT /* Name of the signal */,
  "interface" TEXT /* Interface associated with the signal (e.g., PLB, BRAM) */,
  "signal_type" TEXT /* Type of signal (e.g., O, I, None) */,
  "init_status" TEXT /* Initial status of the signal (e.g., Zeros, Follows SPLB_Rst) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the signal */,
  "signal_frequency" REAL /* Frequency at which the signal is generated or sampled */,
  "signal_amplitude" REAL /* Amplitude of the signal */,
  "signal_phase" REAL /* Phase of the signal */,
  "signal_duration" REAL /* Duration for which the signal is active */,
  "signal_last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the signal */,
  "signal_is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating whether the signal is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("signal_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interfaces" (
  "interface_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interface */,
  "interface_name" TEXT /* Name of the interface (e.g., PLB, BRAM) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the interface */,
  "interface_speed" REAL /* Speed of the interface in Hz */,
  "interface_bandwidth" REAL /* Bandwidth of the interface in bits per second */,
  "interface_last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the interface */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interface_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "signal_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each signal type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the signal type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the signal type */,
  "type_is_input" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating whether the signal type is an input */,
  "type_is_output" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating whether the signal type is an output */,
  "type_is_bidirectional" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating whether the signal type is bidirectional */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "init_statuses" (
  "init_status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each initial status */,
  "init_status_name" TEXT /* Name of the initial status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the initial status */,
  "init_status_is_default" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating whether the initial status is the default status */,
  "init_status_last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the initial status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("init_status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "signal_interfaces" (
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* ID of the signal */,
  "interface_id" INTEGER /* ID of the interface associated with the signal */,
  "interface_priority" INTEGER /* Priority of the interface for the signal */,
  "interface_is_primary" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating whether the interface is the primary interface for the signal */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("signal_id", "interface_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "signal_types_signals" (
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* ID of the signal */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the signal type */,
  "type_priority" INTEGER /* Priority of the signal type for the signal */,
  "type_is_primary" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating whether the signal type is the primary type for the signal */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("signal_id", "type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "init_statuses_signals" (
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* ID of the signal */,
  "init_status_id" INTEGER /* ID of the initial status */,
  "init_status_priority" INTEGER /* Priority of the initial status for the signal */,
  "init_status_is_primary" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating whether the initial status is the primary status for the signal */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("signal_id", "init_status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, analyst, admin) */,
  "user_last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "user_is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating whether the user account is active */,
  "user_created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* ID of the signal being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the signal */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the signal was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, modify) */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "access_duration" REAL /* Duration of the access event in seconds */,
  "access_is_successful" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating whether the access was successful */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_signal_id FOREIGN KEY ("signal_id") REFERENCES signals ("signal_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics data point */,
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* ID of the signal being analyzed */,
  "analytic_type" TEXT /* Type of analytics data (e.g., pattern, anomaly) */,
  "analytic_data" TEXT /* Analytics data (e.g., JSON object) */,
  "analytic_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the analytics data was generated */,
  "analytic_is_anomaly" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating whether the analytics data indicates an anomaly */,
  "analytic_confidence" REAL /* Confidence level of the analytics result */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_signal_id FOREIGN KEY ("signal_id") REFERENCES signals ("signal_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "signal_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* ID of the signal being versioned */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date of the version */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of the version changes */,
  "version_is_major" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating whether the version is a major version */,
  "version_is_minor" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating whether the version is a minor version */,
  "version_is_patch" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating whether the version is a patch version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_signal_versions_signal_id FOREIGN KEY ("signal_id") REFERENCES signals ("signal_id")
)
